easy.title: Write HTML in Two Different Editors
easy.description: Create a simple 'About Me' HTML page using Notepad (or TextEdit) and then recreate the same page using VS Code. Compare the experience — which one was faster and why? The page should have a heading, a paragraph, and a list of your three favorite foods.
easy.starterCode:
My Favorite Foods

Here are three foods I really enjoy:

  • Pizza
  • Sushi
  • Ice Cream
easy.solution:
My Favorite Foods

Here are three foods I really enjoy:

  • Pizza
  • Sushi
  • Ice Cream

medium.title: Master Emmet Abbreviations
medium.description: Write the complete HTML structure for a blog homepage using ONLY Emmet abbreviations in VS Code. The page must include: a header with navigation (Home, About, Blog, Contact), a main section with an article (with h2, p, and an image), an aside with a list of recent posts, and a footer. Write the Emmet string you used and the expanded HTML.
medium.starterCode:
<!-- Type this Emmet abbreviation in VS Code and press Tab:
header>nav>ul>li*4>a[href="#"]{$$$}^^main>article>h2{Blog Post}+p*2+lorem^^aside>h3{Recent Posts}+ul>li*3>a[href="#"]{Post $}^^footer>p{© 2026 My Blog}
-->

<!-- Then manually review and format the expanded HTML -->
medium.solution:
• Home
  • About
  • Blog
  • Contact

Blog Post

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

Recent Posts

  • Post 1
  • Post 2
  • Post 3

© 2026 My Blog

hard.title: Build an HTML Editor Configuration Guide
hard.description: Create an HTML page that serves as a 'My Editor Setup' reference guide. Use only HTML (no CSS) to create a structured document that includes: a table comparing 5 editors (Notepad, VS Code, Sublime Text, Atom, Brackets) with columns for name, platform, price, and best feature; an ordered list of steps taken to set up your VS Code environment; an unordered list of 10 essential VS Code extensions with short descriptions; and a section on keyboard shortcuts displayed as a definition list (<dl>).
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>My Editor Setup Guide</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a comprehensive editor reference guide here -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
HTML Editor Setup Guide

Editor Comparison Table

Editor Platform Price Best Feature
Notepad Windows Free Pre-installed
VS Code Cross-platform Free Extensions & Live Server
Sublime Text Cross-platform $99 (eval free) Speed & Distraction-free
Atom Cross-platform Free GitHub integration
Brackets Cross-platform Free Live Preview

VS Code Setup Steps

  1. Download and install VS Code from code.visualstudio.com
  2. Install Live Server extension for auto-reload
  3. Install Prettier for automatic code formatting
  4. Install Auto Rename Tag for paired tag editing
  5. Configure settings.json for preferred font and theme
  6. Enable autosave (File > Auto Save)
  7. Learn essential Emmet abbreviations

Essential VS Code Extensions

  • Live Server — Launch local server with auto-reload
  • Prettier — Automatic code formatter
  • Auto Rename Tag — Syncs paired HTML tags
  • HTML CSS Support — CSS class autocomplete in HTML
  • Path Intellisense — Autocomplete file paths
  • IntelliSense for CSS — CSS class name completion
  • Bracket Pair Colorizer — Color-coded brackets
  • Material Icon Theme — File icons
  • GitLens — Git blame annotations
  • ESLint — JavaScript linting

Keyboard Shortcuts Reference

Ctrl+S
Save current file
Ctrl+Shift+P
Open Command Palette
Shift+Alt+F
Format document
Ctrl+/
Toggle comment
Alt+Up/Down
Move line up/down
Ctrl+D
Select next occurrence
Ctrl+Shift+K
Delete line

© 2026 My Editor Setup Guide
